Name: tensor_core_aware_kv_cache_compression
Title: Tensor Core-Aware KV Cache Compression for Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing the layout and quantization of the KV cache to exploit Tensor Cores, we can significantly reduce memory bandwidth and improve decoding throughput for long-context LLMs without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Recent work like BitDecoding has explored low-bit KV cache quantization but primarily focuses on CUDA cores. AMP-ViT and SNIP address mixed-precision quantization but do not specifically target Tensor Core optimization for KV cache. Our proposal uniquely combines Tensor Core-aware layout optimization with low-bit quantization, leveraging hardware-specific features to maximize throughput.
Abstract: Long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) face significant memory and bandwidth challenges due to the growing Key-Value (KV) cache during autoregressive decoding. While low-bit quantization reduces memory footprint, existing systems often neglect Tensor Cores, the primary source of compute on modern GPUs. This paper introduces Tensor Core-Aware KV Cache Compression (TCA-KVC), a novel approach that optimizes KV cache layout and quantization to exploit Tensor Cores efficiently. We propose automatic layout induction techniques and warp-level parallelization strategies for dequantization, enabling high-throughput decoding. Our system includes a query transformation module for diverse attention variants and architecture-specific optimizations for the latest GPU generations. Evaluations on LLaMA-3.1-8B with a 128K context demonstrate a 3x reduction in single-batch decoding latency and a 7.5x speedup over FP16 FlashDecoding-v2, showcasing substantial improvements for long-context generation.
Experiments: Compare decoding throughput and latency of TCA-KVC with BitDecoding and FP16 FlashDecoding-v2 on LLaMA-3.1-8B with 128K context.
Measure memory bandwidth utilization and Tensor Core occupancy using nvprof/Nsight traces.
Evaluate the impact of different quantization bit-widths (4-bit, 2-bit) on accuracy and throughput.
Test the system on multiple GPU architectures (Blackwell, Hopper, Ada, Ampere) to ensure robustness.
Perform end-to-end latency races under fixed accuracy budgets, reporting p50/p99 latency and energy per token.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of Tensor Core optimization may vary across different GPU architectures.
Low-bit quantization could introduce accuracy loss, requiring careful tuning of bit-widths.
The proposed layout optimization techniques may not generalize to all attention variants.
Increased complexity in kernel implementation could lead to higher development and maintenance costs.
